zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Gobion於1248年出生在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾霍頓時，她的父親Sir Hugh Gobion38歲，母親Matilda32歲。她於1272年與John de Moretyn II在英國貝德福爾提爾斯沃斯結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1312年在英國英格蘭貝德福爾享年64歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭貝德福爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Gobion于1248年出生在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾霍頓时，她的父亲Sir Hugh Gobion38岁，母亲Matilda32岁。她于1272年与John de Moretyn II在英国貝德福爾提爾斯沃斯结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1312年在英国英格兰貝德福爾享年64岁，埋葬在英国英格兰貝德福爾。

nameMeanings.name: Gobion
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Bhola, Boodram, Mohan, Narine, Parmanand, Pooran, Shiva.  French Andre, Marcelle, Pierre.
French: from a pet form of   Gobert  .

variants.name: Gobert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Antoine, Marie-France, Monique.

French, German, and English (London): from a medieval personal name, Godebert, composed of the ancient Germanic elements gōd ‘good’ or god, got ‘god’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. The name was popularized in England by the Normans, and probably absorbed an Old English form Godbeorht.

variants.name: Goben
meanings: Altered form of Gobin , a surname of English or French origin. In some cases however, the surname Goben (possibly of German origin; see 2 below) was changed to Gobin.
German (Göben): habitational name from Gobbin on Rügen Island (Pomerania) or perhaps from any of several places called Goben in Bavaria.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Gobion was born in 1248, in Horton, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Hugh Gobion, was 38 and her mother, Matilda, was 32. She married John de Moretyn II in 1272, in Tilsworth, Bedfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died in 1312, in Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 64, and was buried in Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Gobion est née en 1248, à Horton, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Hugh Gobion, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Matilda, avait 32 ans. Elle a épousé John de Moretyn II en 1272, à Tilsworth, Bedfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée en 1312, à Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterrée à Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Gobionが1248年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアホールトンで生まれた時，父Sir Hugh Gobionは38歳，母Matildaは32歳であった。1272年にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアティルスウオルスでJohn de Moretyn IIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1312年にイギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアで64歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Gobion(이)가 1248년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 호르턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Hugh Gobion(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Matilda(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 1272년 영국 베드퍼드실르 틸스워스에서 John de Moretyn II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1312년 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Gobion родилась в 1248 г. в местности хауртон, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir Hugh Gobion было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John de Moretyn II в 1272 г. в местности тилсворт, бедфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла в 1312 г. в местности бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 64 лет и была похоронена в местности бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.
